purpose: add_closed_world_predicate's semantic effect: on a two-Friends-edge graph with suspicious(Mary) known [1,1] and suspicious never stated for John/Justin, the rule safe(x) <-1 Friends(x,y), suspicious(x):[0,0] fires for John and Justin only when 'suspicious' is closed-world — the Friends clause grounds x, then is_satisfied_node's CWA branch (interpretation.py:1763-1770) treats the missing/unknown [0,1] label as [0,0], satisfying the [0,0] clause, while Mary's known [1,1] still fails it. A second identical add is a set-level no-op (pyreason.py:1130) — same downstream results with the duplicate applied. closed-world-off is the no-CWA twin (no safe rows at all); the effect is deferred to reason(), observed through the trace, never a stored value.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_closed_world_predicate happy-basic/bound-duplicate; pyreason.py:1122-1130, :1613-1617 and interpretation.py:1757-1775 at the pin; semantics screened live 2026-07-07
